content.text:
Recent top discussions on X (formerly Twitter) about AI agent skills, skill systems, capabilities, and tool calling focus on practical challenges like tool discovery, efficient loading (e.g., progressive/MCP gateways vs. upfront tool dumps), and primitives that empower models (conditional context, precise instructions).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018860160945635619)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018010720789844284)

Key highlights:
- **Decomposing agent ecosystems**: Proposals to separate searchable "agents" (with APIs), "skills" (modular tools or MD for purchase/absorption), and "objects" (files), beyond social networks.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2017884412352794810)
- **Agent primitives like tool-calling, MCP, skills**: Successful ones let models conditionally load context and provide exact prompt needs.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018860160945635619)
- **MCP gateways for efficiency**: Rust-based meta-tools (search/inspect/call/list) for progressive discovery, reducing tokens from 18k to 50—but critics note agents struggle with reliable tool discovery, favoring task-specific subagents.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018010720789844284)

These threads (from Feb 1-4, 2026) reflect ongoing experimentation in agent architectures, emphasizing composability over brute-force prompting.
